text:
Epilepsy Centre

Neurosciences Division


From: Dr Priya Shah (KING'S COLLEGE HOSPITAL NHS FOUNDATION TRUST)
Sent: 10 September 2016 10:15
To: neuro.epilepsyclinic@nhs.net
Cc: Dr Michael O’Connor (KING'S COLLEGE HOSPITAL NHS FOUNDATION TRUST); Dr Emily Davies (KING'S COLLEGE HOSPITAL NHS FOUNDATION TRUST)
Subject: Clinic review and dietary therapy update

Hi Priya

Epilepsy Diagnosis:

Focal epilepsy with impaired awareness seizures, likely temporal lobe onset (patient has focal epilepsy)

Present Medication:

1. Lamotrigine 200 mg twice daily
2. Lacosamide 100 mg twice daily

Dietary Therapy:

Patient is following a ketogenic/modified diet supervised externally by a specialist dietetics service. The patient provided a written record of dietary parameters and daily intake logs, summarised as follows: ketogenic ratio targeted at 2:1 most days (fat:combined protein and carbohydrate), total carbohydrate restricted to 20–30 g/day, protein adjusted to approximately 1.0 g/kg/day, with emphasis on whole-food fats (olive oil, avocado, nuts) and measured portions. The patient reports using home blood ketone testing (beta-hydroxybutyrate typically 1.2–2.0 mmol/L) and maintaining adequate hydration and electrolyte supplementation (magnesium and sodium as advised). No hypoglycaemic episodes reported. Diet is overseen remotely with monthly check-ins; vitamin D and selenium supplementation documented.

Present Seizure Frequency:

The patient reports an ongoing pattern of 21 to 28 seizures per month, predominantly brief focal impaired awareness events lasting 30–90 seconds with post-ictal fatigue. There are occasional clusters around significant sleep disruption. No generalised tonic–clonic seizures documented in the past six months.

Plan of Action:

- Continue current antiepileptic regimen for now while we obtain updated external dietary clinic correspondence to ensure coordinated care.
- Arrange routine bloods (FBC, U&E, LFTs) and bone profile; check vitamin D level and fasting lipid profile given ongoing ketogenic/modified diet.
- Request a copy of the dietary plan and recent monitoring results from the supervising dietetics team (with patient consent) to align targets and avoid duplication.
- Reinforce adherence to regular sleep schedule and seizure safety measures. The patient has recorded triggers as sleep loss and high cognitive load; diary to be continued.
- MRI brain (epilepsy protocol) from last year showed left mesial temporal sclerosis as per patient report; we have requested the imaging and report for local review.
- Follow-up in three months in clinic to reassess frequency and tolerability; earlier review if seizure characteristics change or adverse effects emerge.

With best wishes

entities.text: The patient reports an ongoing pattern of 21 to 28 seizures per month

No other conflicting frequencies are provided. Apply the rule to use explicit numeric expressions and select the stated monthly range. Convert directly to the allowed output format as a range per month.
